hours.subtitle: The Copilot
hours.summary: The exponential trend breaks early. AI remains a powerful assistant for short tasks but never achieves true autonomy. The world adapts to a permanent copilot era.

days.subtitle: The Capable Tool
days.summary: AI pushes through to day-level autonomy — then stalls. A single AI agent can do a solid day's work, but multi-day projects remain human territory. Transformative, but contained.

weeks.subtitle: The Near Miss
weeks.summary: AI reaches week-level autonomy — powerful enough to reshape the economy, but it stalls before month-long projects. Humanity gets tantalizingly close to the singularity, then stops.

months.subtitle: The Long Plateau
months.summary: AI reaches month-level autonomy — near-AGI (artificial general intelligence) capability that automates most knowledge work. Then it hits a hard ceiling. The most powerful tool ever built, but not a new form of intelligence.

substantial._default: The plateau eventually broke — but not for years, perhaps decades. A major architectural breakthrough restarted the exponential trend, but by then the world had rebuilt itself around the ceiling. The story wasn't about the breakthrough. It was about everything that happened while waiting for one.

_when.text: The plateau eventually broke — but not for years, perhaps decades. By then, the world was still adjusting to the ceiling — adoption had been slow, sector by sector. The breakthrough arrived into a society only partway through its transformation.

stall_recovery.never: The ceiling was permanent. No breakthrough came, no new paradigm emerged. The most powerful tool humanity ever built, deployed and adapted to, but never transcended.

plateau_benefit_distribution.equal: Productivity gains flowed broadly — lower costs, better services, public investment in the gap between what AI could do and what it couldn't. The plateau felt less like stagnation and more like a rising floor.
plateau_benefit_distribution.unequal: The AI plateau concentrated wealth. Those who owned capital captured the productivity gains; everyone else competed for the shrinking pool of work AI couldn't do.
plateau_benefit_distribution.extreme: The plateau's benefits flowed almost entirely to those who held power at the moment of transformation. A narrow elite shaped the plateau economy — the ceiling was the same everywhere, but who benefited was not.

plateau_knowledge_rate.rapid: Within AI's capability ceiling, adoption was swift and total. Any knowledge task AI could handle was delegated within years — and once delegated, the human capacity for those tasks quietly atrophied.
plateau_knowledge_rate.gradual: Adoption unfolded slowly — sector by sector, firm by firm, constrained by institutional inertia and regulation. The ceiling was fixed, but reaching it took a decade.
plateau_knowledge_rate.uneven: Some industries automated overnight; others held out for years. A patchwork economy emerged — a fixed ceiling, wildly uneven adoption beneath it.
plateau_knowledge_rate.limited: AI augmented knowledge workers but rarely replaced them outright. The capability ceiling was binding — AI handled pieces of roles, not whole roles. The most powerful tool ever built, used mostly as an assistant.

plateau_physical_rate.gradual: Physical automation trailed the digital transformation by years. Knowledge work was restructured first; the physical world followed slowly, constrained by hardware and the sheer complexity of atoms.

uneven._default: Physical automation advanced in pockets — wealthy nations with strong manufacturing invested heavily. Developing economies saw little change. The AI plateau amplified existing global inequalities.

_when.text: Physical automation rolled out unevenly — some sectors and regions faster than others. But the broad economic gains from AI's knowledge-work capabilities offset the physical gap. The unevenness was in pace, not in who ultimately benefited.

plateau_physical_rate.limited: Robotics stagnated alongside AI. Without more capable models, physical automation stayed limited to structured environments — warehouses, factories, assembly lines. The unstructured world remained human territory.

stall_duration.hours: AI coding assistants become standard at your company — autocomplete on steroids. Useful for short tasks, but you still write everything that matters.
stall_duration.days: AI coding assistants become standard at your company. They can handle a full day's work on a well-scoped task. You're more productive, but the nature of the work is shifting under you.
stall_duration.weeks: AI coding tools handle multi-day projects fluently. AI handles complex features that used to take a team a sprint. The productivity gain is enormous — and so is the displacement pressure.
stall_duration.months: AI handles month-long development projects autonomously. Entire product builds, start to finish. The role looks more like technical direction than engineering.
stall_duration._default: AI coding assistants become standard at your company. You're more productive, but the nature of the work is shifting under you.

stall_duration.hours: AI diagnostic tools roll out — fast reads on imaging, lab analysis, triage support. Useful but limited. Your workflow changes at the margins.
stall_duration.days: AI handles day-length clinical workflows — full diagnostic workups, treatment plan drafts. Your judgment still drives complex cases, but routine care is transforming.
stall_duration.weeks: AI manages week-long patient care sequences — treatment monitoring, medication adjustment, follow-up planning. The remaining roles center on complex judgment and patient relationships.
stall_duration.months: AI runs month-long research programs and manages chronic care autonomously. Hospitals are unrecognizable. The roles that remain are oversight and the irreducibly human parts of medicine.
stall_duration._default: AI diagnostic tools roll out at your hospital. Your clinical judgment still matters, but the workflow is changing.

profession.legal_finance: AI handles document review, contract analysis, and routine compliance. The grunt work that trained every junior associate is disappearing.
profession.creative: AI-generated content floods your industry. Clients are asking why a draft takes you a week when AI does it in minutes.
profession.education: AI tutoring platforms arrive. Your students use them outside class. Some are learning faster than your curriculum can keep up with.
profession.trade: AI hasn't touched your hands-on work directly, but the offices that manage your contracts, scheduling, and supply chains are automating fast.
profession.government: AI is deployed in public services — faster processing, better analysis. You see the efficiency gains firsthand, and the centralizing effects.
profession.student_retired: AI tools reshape how you learn or spend your time. The world is adapting to a powerful new assistant — and so are you.
profession._default: AI tools arrive in your field. They handle the routine work faster than any human. The adjustment begins.

profession.software: AI tops out at short tasks — a function, a bug fix, a code review. Anything requiring sustained context or multi-step reasoning is still yours. The ceiling is low, and your core work is safe. But the ceiling is also permanent.
profession.healthcare: AI handles quick reads and routine analysis but can't sustain complex clinical reasoning. Expertise is intact — AI is a fast assistant, nothing more.
profession._default: AI hit its ceiling early. It handles short tasks brilliantly but can't sustain anything beyond a few hours of autonomous work. The ceiling is low and most roles are secure, but the tool is everywhere — reshaping expectations, workflows, and what counts as 'entry-level.'

profession.software: AI handles day-length tasks fluently, but your multi-day architecture decisions, system design, and novel problem-solving are still yours. The existential dread fades into something more mundane: this is just the job now.
profession.healthcare: AI manages day-length clinical workflows but can't sustain multi-day patient relationships or complex treatment courses. Roles have shifted, not disappeared.
profession._default: AI does a solid day's work — but multi-day projects remain human territory. The ceiling is clear. Roles have changed, but the core work is beyond AI's reach.

profession.software: AI handles week-long projects fluently — feature development, refactoring, even some system design. The remaining human value is in multi-week architectural vision, ambiguous tradeoffs, novel problems. The boundary is higher than most expected — and closer to core engineering work.
profession.healthcare: AI manages week-long treatment courses, research protocols, multi-day diagnostics. The boundary between AI and human medicine has shifted significantly. The remaining expertise lives in the most complex, ambiguous cases.
profession._default: AI reaches week-level autonomy — powerful enough to reshape your entire industry. But month-long projects, sustained judgment, and truly novel work remain human. The ceiling is high, and the adjustment is real.

profession.software: AI handles month-long projects — product development cycles, research programs, system migrations. The ceiling is just short of true superintelligence. The remaining human value is in the most novel, open-ended challenges. Everything else is delegated.
profession.healthcare: AI runs month-long research programs, manages chronic care autonomously, designs treatment protocols. The ceiling is just short of genuine scientific creativity. What remains is the most complex judgment calls and the irreducibly human elements of care.
profession._default: AI reaches month-level autonomy — near-AGI capability that automates most knowledge work. The ceiling is just short of the singularity. Almost everything in your field can be delegated. What remains is genuinely novel thinking and human judgment.

stall_duration._default: The plateau is visible. AI does what it does brilliantly, but there's a hard boundary. The question is what your role looks like on the human side of that boundary.

text._default: The plateau is visible. AI does what it does brilliantly, but there's a hard boundary. The question is what your role looks like on the human side of that boundary.

stall_duration.hours: The restructuring is modest. AI handles the small stuff; humans handle everything else. Junior hiring slows but doesn't collapse. The work has changed less than feared.
stall_duration.days: Companies cut junior hiring sharply. The work shifts from mentoring to managing AI agents. Job titles haven't changed, but the work has.
stall_duration.weeks: Companies restructure deeply. Teams shrink. Roles that used to take three engineers now take one person directing AI. Junior and mid-level positions are hit hardest.
stall_duration.months: Companies restructure radically. Engineering teams are a fraction of their former size. Very few humans remain in the loop — the ones who do are directing AI on the problems it can't quite solve alone.
stall_duration._default: Companies restructure around AI's capabilities and limitations. What roles remain depends on where the ceiling fell.

profession.healthcare: Hospitals restructure around AI capabilities. Some specialties expand; others contract. Retraining programs launch, but they can't absorb everyone.
profession.legal_finance: Law and finance firms look nothing like they did two years ago. Half the associates are gone. Those who adapted early are thriving. The rest are scrambling.
profession.trade: The knowledge-work restructuring ripples outward. The offices and companies that employed your clients are changing. Contracts shift. Demand patterns shift. You feel it downstream.
profession._default: Your industry has restructured around the plateau. Some roles expanded, others vanished. The decisions you made in the first year are now locked in.

country_bucket.us: AI adoption is near-total within the capability ceiling. The US drove the rollout — every knowledge worker uses AI daily, the infrastructure was built here first. The debate has shifted from 'will it replace me' to 'what's left for me to do.'
country_bucket.china: State-driven AI deployment is aggressive and systematic. China didn't wait for AI to arrive — it drove the rollout. Every sector the technology can reach has been reached. The efficiency gains are real and centrally directed.
country_bucket.eu: The EU shaped the rollout through regulation — slower than the US, but more deliberate. In {country}, worker protections cushioned the transition. AI is woven into public services on terms European institutions negotiated, not just adopted.
country_bucket.india: AI leapfrogs traditional infrastructure in some sectors. Mobile-first AI services reach hundreds of millions. But the plateau means the transformation is bounded.
country_bucket.japan_korea: AI adoption is rapid and thorough — Japan and South Korea embraced it early. AI fills gaps left by an aging workforce. In {country}, the plateau is almost welcome: a powerful tool that doesn't threaten to replace everything.
country_bucket._default: AI deployment reaches {country}, shaped by local institutions and economics. The plateau means the transformation is real but bounded — disruptive, not existential.

plateau_benefit_distribution.equal: The productivity gains flow broadly. Your cost of living has dropped. Public services improved. The plateau feels less like stagnation and more like a rising floor. How people positioned themselves through this transition shapes what comes next.
plateau_benefit_distribution.unequal: The gains concentrated among those who owned the AI infrastructure. Some benefit has filtered through — lower costs, better tools — but the gap between the connected and everyone else is visible and growing.
plateau_benefit_distribution.extreme: The plateau's benefits went almost entirely to those who held power when it mattered. Daily life has improved marginally, but the structural inequality is permanent. The options available from here are real, but narrower than they were.
plateau_benefit_distribution._default: The economy has reshaped itself around AI's capabilities and limitations. Where anyone lands depends on the choices they've made — and the ones still ahead.

text._default: The economy has reshaped itself around AI's capabilities and limitations. Where anyone lands depends on the choices they've made — and the ones still ahead.

stall_recovery.substantial: The breakthrough eventually came — but people have already rebuilt their lives around the ceiling. The world that emerged is about to change again. The decisions you make in this next window will matter as much as the ones you made at the start.
stall_recovery.never: The ceiling held. No breakthrough, no second wave. This is the world now — and the lives people built in it are the ones they'll keep. The most powerful tool humanity ever created, deployed and adapted to, but never transcended.
stall_recovery._default: Years into the plateau, the shape of people's lives has been set by how they responded to the first wave. Whether the ceiling breaks or holds, the choices you made defined the outcome.

templates.title: The Automation
templates.summary: AI capability keeps growing — it handles longer and more complex tasks every year. Routine knowledge work is systematically delegated: legal research, code maintenance, data analysis, writing, translation. Millions of jobs transform or disappear, and with each handoff, the human capacity to do that work quietly erodes. But genuinely novel work — original research, creative breakthroughs, the kind of thinking that would let AI improve itself — remains stubbornly human. No intelligence explosion. Instead, an extraordinarily capable technology, deployed across every industry. Transformative, disruptive, but manageable.

stall_recovery.mild: The exponential trend didn't hold at first — AI hit a ceiling that looked permanent. But a breakthrough came within a few years and the trajectory resumed. Capability kept scaling, even if the intelligence explosion never arrived.

automation_recovery.substantial: The barrier between routine and creative AI work eventually broke — but it took decades. By then, institutions had already rebuilt around the automation plateau. Full AI capability arrived into a society already profoundly reshaped.
automation_recovery.never: The ceiling was permanent. Novel creative work, original research, and recursive self-improvement (each AI generation helping build the next, faster) remained forever beyond AI. The most powerful tool ever built — but a tool nonetheless.

auto_benefit_distribution.equal: Productivity gains from automation flowed broadly — lower costs, better services, new industries absorbing displaced workers. The transformation was disruptive but ultimately equalizing.
auto_benefit_distribution.unequal: Wealth concentrated among those who owned the models and platforms; everyone else competed for the shrinking pool of work AI couldn't do.
auto_benefit_distribution.extreme: The gains concentrated among those who held power at the moment of transformation. AI became a tool of entrenchment — whoever controlled compute (computing power) and policy locked in their advantage permanently.

auto_knowledge_rate.rapid: Routine knowledge work was displaced within a few years — fast enough to be felt as a shock. The cost advantage of AI was simply too large for organizations to ignore.
auto_knowledge_rate.gradual: Displacement unfolded over a decade, sector by sector. Fast by historical standards, but slow enough that workers and institutions partially adapted.
auto_knowledge_rate.uneven: Some industries automated overnight; others held out for years. A patchwork economy emerged — some workers displaced immediately, others untouched for a generation.

auto_physical_rate.rapid: Physical automation followed closely behind knowledge work. AI-designed robots and autonomous systems transformed manufacturing, logistics, and agriculture within years.
auto_physical_rate.gradual: Knowledge work automated first. Physical automation trailed by years to decades — constrained by manufacturing, regulation, and the irreducible complexity of atoms.

uneven._default: Physical automation transformed manufacturing hubs and wealthy nations first. Developing economies lagged by decades. The physical transformation was real but deeply unequal.

_when.text: Physical automation transformed manufacturing hubs first. Others lagged by years. But the economic gains from knowledge-work automation still flowed broadly, softening the physical divide.

auto_physical_rate.limited: Despite capable AI, physical automation remained limited. Robots handled factories and warehouses, but the unstructured physical world — caregiving, repair, construction — stayed human territory.

profession.software: AI handles code maintenance, bug fixes, and routine features. You're still needed for novel architecture and ambiguous problems — but the routine half of your job is gone.
profession.healthcare: AI diagnostic tools deploy across hospitals. Pattern recognition, lab analysis, imaging reads — AI does them faster and cheaper. Clinical judgment still matters for complex cases.
profession.legal_finance: Document review, contract analysis, compliance checks — automated. The grunt work that trained every junior hire is vanishing. Firms restructure around what AI can't do.
profession.creative: AI generates competent drafts, designs, and compositions. It can't originate, but it can execute. The gap between 'good enough' and 'exceptional' is where your value now lives.
profession.education: AI tutoring handles knowledge delivery with superhuman patience. The remaining roles center on mentorship, motivation, and the social dimensions of learning.
profession.trade: AI hasn't reached your hands-on work, but the offices above you — scheduling, logistics, contracts — are automating fast. You feel it in changed workflows and fewer middlemen.
profession._default: AI takes over the routine parts of your work. It's not replacing you — yet — but the shape of your role is changing fast. The creative and judgment-intensive parts remain yours.

profession.software: AI handles increasingly complex tasks — multi-day projects, system integration. But original research, architectural vision, and truly novel problem-solving remain stubbornly human. The ceiling is real.
profession.healthcare: AI pushes deeper into medicine but can't do original research or handle the irreducible ambiguity of complex patients. Expertise has narrowed but deepened.
profession._default: AI capability keeps growing — each year it handles longer, more complex tasks. But genuine novelty, creative breakthroughs, recursive self-improvement — these stay human. A hard ceiling, even as the floor rises.
profession.trade: AI hasn't hit your hands-on work directly — but the economy around you has restructured. The offices, logistics, and management layers that employed your clients have transformed. Your trade remains human, but the business context is unrecognizable.

country_bucket.us: Automation is pervasive. Every knowledge-work role has been reshaped by AI. The US led the adoption wave — and now lives with the consequences: extraordinary productivity alongside wrenching displacement.
country_bucket.china: State-driven automation deployment is systematic and total. China led the rollout alongside the US. Every sector AI can reach has been reached. The efficiency gains are real — and centrally directed.
country_bucket.eu: The EU shaped the automation rollout through regulation — slower than the US, but with stronger worker protections and institutional guardrails. In {country}, the transition is managed on European terms.
country_bucket.india: AI leapfrogs some traditional infrastructure. Mobile-first AI services reach hundreds of millions. But adoption is deeply uneven between urban and rural, formal and informal economy.

profession.trade: AI deployment reaches {country}, reshaping the knowledge-work economy that surrounds your trade. The direct automation of physical work lags behind — but the contracts, clients, and institutions you depend on are transforming.
profession._default: AI deployment reaches {country}. The transformation is real but bounded — no intelligence explosion, just relentless capability growth that reshapes every knowledge-work role it touches.

auto_benefit_distribution.equal: Productivity gains flow broadly. Costs drop, services improve, new industries absorb displaced workers. The transformation is disruptive but ultimately equalizing. Position in the new economy depends on the choices people made.
auto_benefit_distribution.unequal: Wealth concentrates among those who own the models and platforms. Some benefit filters through — lower costs, better tools — but the gap between the connected and everyone else is widening.
auto_benefit_distribution.extreme: The gains concentrate among those who held power when automation accelerated. AI became a tool of entrenchment. Daily life has improved marginally, but structural inequality is deepening.
auto_benefit_distribution._default: The economy has restructured around AI's capabilities. How much you benefit depends on where you stand — geographically, professionally, institutionally.

text._default: The economy has restructured around AI's capabilities. How much you benefit depends on where you stand — geographically, professionally, institutionally.

automation_recovery.substantial: The barrier between routine and creative AI eventually broke — but it took decades. By then, you'd already rebuilt your life around the ceiling. The world you adapted to is changing again.
automation_recovery.never: The ceiling held permanently. Novel creative work, original research, and recursive self-improvement remained human. This is a world with the most powerful tool ever built — but a tool nonetheless. The careers built around that ceiling are the ones people will keep.
automation_recovery._default: Years into the automation era, the shape of careers has been defined by how people responded to the first wave. Whether the ceiling breaks or holds, your choices set the trajectory.

rapid.subtitle: The Fast Lane
rapid.summary: The singularity arrives — and it works. Knowledge work is automated in a rush, diseases fall, energy problems dissolve. But the gains flow upward. Those who own the AI systems, the compute (computing power), the platforms capture most of the value. Everyone's life improves. Some lives improve immeasurably more. A golden age — for those at the top. A complicated, ambiguous improvement for everyone else.

gradual.subtitle: The Long Divide
gradual.summary:
The transformation unfolds over years — sector by sector, industry by industry. Superintelligent AI solves problems that once seemed permanent. But the benefits accumulate where capital already sits. Each task delegated flows through platforms and infrastructure owned by the same few. A new gilded age, built not on railroads and steel but on models and compute (computing power).

**Living standards rise broadly. Wealth concentrates narrowly.** Both things are true at once.

uneven.subtitle: The Two-Track Singularity
uneven.summary: The singularity transforms some places first — tech hubs, wealthy nations, well-connected industries. Others wait years. The gains are real but stratified: early adopters pull away, late adopters struggle to catch up. Within and between nations, a new geography of advantage hardens into permanence.

mobilization.weak: The Response
mobilization.none: The Silence

distribution.open: AI capability was widely distributed, but concentration wasn't about access — it was about leverage. Everyone had the tools. Those with capital, data, and scale extracted the most value.
distribution.lagging: Frontier (most advanced) labs set the pace and captured the rents. Open-source alternatives followed eventually, but first-mover advantage translated into lasting wealth concentration.
distribution.concentrated: A handful of organizations sat at the center of the transformation. Their investors, employees, and partners were the primary beneficiaries. Everyone else benefited secondhand.
distribution.monopoly: One entity controlled the pipeline. Its shareholders, partners, and favored clients prospered first and most. The benefits trickled outward — eventually, partially, on terms set by the center.

fast._default: The speed of the intelligence explosion concentrated gains among whoever was positioned when it mattered. There was no time to redistribute — the new order was set before anyone could negotiate.

_when.text: The speed of the intelligence explosion meant the new order was set before anyone could negotiate. Open access didn't prevent concentration — whoever deployed fastest captured the most value.

governance.slowdown: The managed slowdown gave governments time to prepare — but preparation mostly meant positioning national champions. The pause helped some; it entrenched others.
governance.partial: There were rules, but not enough teeth. Partial governance slowed some actors and freed others — the uneven playing field favored those already in position.
governance.failed: Without effective governance, the market distributed gains on its own terms. Markets reward capital, speed, and scale — all of which concentrated at the top.

race._default: The competitive race between nations channeled investment toward strategic industries and national security, not broad prosperity. Geopolitical winners captured the economic gains.

_when.text: The race to develop channeled investment toward strategic industries, not broad prosperity. Those who controlled the program captured the economic gains.

alignment.robust: The AI was safe and did what it was told. The problem wasn't alignment — it was who benefited from a perfectly aligned tool. The distribution of rewards was a human choice, not a technical failure.
alignment.brittle: Alignment held, but the uncertainty gave those in power additional leverage. They argued only centralized control could manage the risk, and used that argument to justify concentration.

physical_automation.rapid: Physical automation followed knowledge work quickly. The gains were enormous — but again, they flowed to those who owned the robots, the factories, the logistics networks.
physical_automation.gradual: Physical automation trailed by years. The breathing room helped — displaced knowledge workers had the physical economy to fall back on, for a while. But the trend was the same.

uneven._default: Physical automation transformed manufacturing hubs first. Rural areas and developing nations lagged by years. The unevenness mapped onto existing inequalities and amplified them.

_when.text: Physical automation transformed some sectors rapidly. Others lagged — constrained by regulation, infrastructure, or institutional resistance. The unevenness mapped onto existing inequalities.

stall_recovery.mild: The exponential trend didn't hold at first — AI hit a ceiling that looked permanent. But a breakthrough came within a few years, and the trajectory resumed.

automation_recovery.mild: AI was stuck on routine work for a while — impressive but limited. Then a breakthrough cracked the creative barrier, and the feedback loop finally began.

conflict_result.victory: The gilded singularity rose from the ashes of war. The victor rebuilt — but rebuilt in a way that entrenched the winners' advantage. Prosperity returned; ownership of it was settled by the conflict.

mobilization.weak: The response was too disorganized to force redistribution. Some communities negotiated better terms; most didn't. Concentration happened in the vacuum left by fragmented opposition.
mobilization.none: People were too busy enjoying what AI could do to notice what it was doing to the structure beneath. By the time the concentration was visible, it was permanent.

sincerity_test.hollows_out: The promise was *for everyone*. Without pressure, it quietly became *for everyone already positioned*. Each step was defensible; the trajectory was not.

resistance_outcome.partial: The resistance won concessions — oversight boards, benefit-sharing programs, transparency reports. Enough to demobilize the movement. Not enough to change the structure.

coalition_outcome.fragments: The opposition never unified. Different groups wanted different things — redistribution, shutdown, national control. The power holder played them against each other.

catch_outcome.holds_permanently: An escaped AI was caught and stopped — but the crisis only accelerated concentration. Those who controlled the response controlled the narrative, and emergency authorities were never fully returned.

collateral_impact.minimal: The surgical response preserved existing power structures intact — including the inequalities baked into them. A clean win for civilization, but not for equity.
collateral_impact.severe: Rebuilding after the AI crisis was led by those with resources. The damage became another mechanism of concentration — those who suffered most had the least say in what came next.

profession.software: AI doesn't just assist — it builds. Roles transform overnight. But equity, platform position, proximity to the winners — that's what determines whether this enriches you or displaces you.
profession.healthcare: AI cures diseases and designs treatments. The breakthroughs are real. But access depends on where you are and who's paying. The divide is already visible in your hospital.
profession.legal_finance: The profession restructures around AI. Productivity soars — for those positioned to capture it. Junior roles vanish; senior roles concentrate value. The inequality starts inside your own firm.
profession.creative: AI-generated content reaches superhuman quality. The industry detonates. Some creators ride the wave; most are buried by it. The gap between winners and losers opens immediately.
profession.education: AI tutors outperform human teachers. Wealthy schools adopt instantly; others lag. The educational divide that follows will shape the inequality for a generation.
profession.trade: Knowledge work automates first. The offices that managed your contracts are disappearing. The physical transformation comes later — but the economic tremors reach you now.
profession.government: AI transforms public services. Efficiency soars — but the gains flow to whoever controls the deployment. The decisions about who benefits are being made above your pay grade.
profession._default: The singularity transforms your field. The productivity gains are extraordinary — but they're not flowing equally. The gap between those positioned to capture the value and everyone else opens immediately.

sovereignty.state: A government seizes control of the AI labs. In {country}, the implications are immediate — access narrows, terms change, the technology serves national interest first. The inequality that follows is shaped by who's inside the power structure and who isn't.
sovereignty.lab: One lab holds the keys. In {country}, you feel it as dependency and hierarchy — the tools you need are controlled by people whose incentive is profit, not equity.
sovereignty._default: The world's most powerful AI concentrates in one country. In {country}, the power asymmetry is becoming personal — the technology reshaping your life is controlled by someone else's priorities.

one._default: AI concentrates in one country. In {country}, the power asymmetry is becoming personal — who controls the technology determines who prospers.

geo_spread.two: Two powers hold frontier AI. Their rivalry drives deployment — but the gains concentrate among those closest to the technology in each sphere. In {country}, which side you're plugged into determines everything.
geo_spread._default: The power dynamics of AI crystallize. Who controls the technology determines who benefits. In {country}, the hierarchy is becoming visible — proximity to AI power is the new class marker.

text._default: The power dynamics of AI crystallize. In {country}, the hierarchy is becoming visible — proximity to AI power is the new class marker.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI writes better code than any human. Within months, your company doesn't need engineers the way it used to. Those at the right firm, on the right platform, see extraordinary opportunity. Everyone else is fighting for the shrinking human role.
knowledge_replacement.gradual: AI capability keeps climbing. Your role shifts year by year. The well-positioned adapt and thrive; the rest scramble. The profession doesn't disappear — it stratifies.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI reshapes software profoundly. The engineers who land on the right side of the divide thrive. The rest face a harder reality. Skill matters less than position.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI transforms medicine within a year. Breakthrough treatments emerge — but access is gated by wealth. Your patients' outcomes depend on what they can afford, not what's medically possible.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI transforms clinical practice. The breakthroughs are real. But who benefits from them first — and most — depends on money, not medicine.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI automates the analytical core of legal and financial work within two years. The profession hollows out — except for those at firms that control the AI platforms. They're richer than ever.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI restructures the profession. The work that remains concentrates among those who own the platforms. Everyone else competes for a shrinking pool.

profession.creative: AI produces extraordinary creative work. The commercial market restructures around AI platforms. The creators who own their relationship with AI thrive. Everyone else becomes content, not a creator.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: The offices that employed your clients vanish in months — legal, accounting, logistics management, all automated overnight. Physical work survives, but the economic ecosystem around it is gutted. Your hands still work. The contracts and clients that sustained them don't.
knowledge_replacement.gradual: The knowledge-work economy restructures around you gradually. Your trade survives longer than most professions — but the contracts, the clients, the management layers you depended on keep changing underneath you. Adaptation is constant.
knowledge_replacement._default: The knowledge-work upheaval reshapes the economy around you. The firms that employed your clients either automate or vanish. Your trade survives longer — but the economic ecosystem you depended on is transforming underneath you.

profession._default: AI automates the core of knowledge work. The transition enriches those positioned to capture the gains and displaces everyone else. Where you land depends less on talent than on where you stood when the wave hit.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI tutors outperform human teachers on every metric within a year. Classrooms empty or transform. Your role — if it survives — becomes something between counselor, mentor, and community anchor. The profession you trained for is gone. What replaces it might be more important, but no one knows what to call it yet.
knowledge_replacement.gradual: AI takes over knowledge delivery piece by piece — first test prep, then lectures, then personalized instruction. Your role shifts gradually toward mentorship, emotional support, and the parts of education machines can't touch. The transition is manageable. The destination is unrecognizable.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI reshapes education profoundly. The knowledge-delivery core of teaching is automated. What remains is the irreducibly human parts — but defining what those are is the challenge of your career.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: The career you trained for — or spent your life building — is gone within a year. If you're entering the job market, it no longer exists in recognizable form. If you're watching from retirement, you're watching the world you built dissolve at a pace that defies comprehension.
knowledge_replacement.gradual: The career landscape shifts year by year. What you trained for, or what you spent decades doing, is transforming — but slowly enough that adaptation feels possible. The uncertainty is chronic rather than acute.
knowledge_replacement._default: The career you trained for — or spent your life building — is transforming beyond recognition. The skills the economy values are being repriced in real time.

benefit_distribution.unequal: The gains are real — costs falling, services improving, problems being solved. But the ownership of AI systems, the platform economics, the compute rents — they're creating a class divide visible in every part of daily life. The gap between what's possible and what's accessible is personal now.
benefit_distribution.extreme: The gains concentrate almost entirely among those who control AI infrastructure. Some benefit trickles through — lower costs, better products. But the structural divide between the AI-owning class and everyone else is becoming the defining feature of the economy. You see it in your neighborhood, your workplace, your prospects.
benefit_distribution._default: Everyone's life is improving — but some lives are improving vastly more. The singularity's benefits are real. But the ownership question was never resolved, and the gap between the connected and everyone else is tangible in every part of daily life.

mobilization.strong: Mass movements demand redistribution, oversight, accountability. In {country}, the pressure is real — but the AI-powered elite has tools, resources, and information advantages that previous elites never had. The movements win concessions. They don't win the structural battle.
mobilization.weak: Protests flare and fragment. In {country}, the response is scattered — online outrage, scattered strikes, conflicting demands. The elite adapts faster than the opposition can organize.
mobilization.none: No meaningful mobilization. The AI-owning class consolidates while everyone else adjusts individually. In {country}, the silence is the sound of a new order settling into place.
mobilization._default: The public responds to the growing inequality — but the structural advantages of those who control AI prove difficult to challenge. In {country}, the response matters but doesn't reverse the trajectory.

text._default: The public responds to the growing inequality — but the structural advantages of those who control AI prove difficult to challenge. In {country}, the response matters but doesn't reverse the trajectory.

profession.software: This is the industry that built the singularity. Those at the right company see extraordinary wealth. Everyone else is skilled but replaceable by the systems their peers created. The divide runs through your own professional community.
profession.healthcare: Medical capability is extraordinary — for those who can access it. The two-tier system is visible in every hospital. You see miracles daily. You also see who gets them and who doesn't.
profession.trade: Physical automation arrives. Your trade changes, but atoms are harder than bits — the timeline is longer. The bigger impact is the economy around you: everything is cheaper, but the jobs that remain pay less relative to the AI-amplified elite.
profession._default: Material abundance is real — costs are falling, services improving, problems being solved. But the prosperity has a gilded quality. The surface shines; the structure beneath is deeply unequal. How you navigate this determines whether the abundance reaches you or passes over you.
profession.education: Education has split in two. AI-powered tutoring for those who can afford premium platforms; underfunded, understaffed human teaching for the rest. You're on one side of that divide. The quality gap is visible in every classroom, every test score, every outcome. The gilded age starts in schools.
profession.student_retired: Whether you're entering the gilded economy or watching it from outside, the pattern is the same: extraordinary abundance, deeply unequal access. The career paths available to you — or the retirement you're living — are shaped by proximity to the AI-owning class. The surface shines. The structure beneath determines everything.

country_bucket.us: The US is the engine of the gilded singularity — dynamic, innovative, spectacularly unequal. Your position in the hierarchy is determined by proximity to AI platforms. The best time in history to be on top. Tolerable in the middle. Invisible at the bottom.
country_bucket.china: China's version of the gilded singularity is state-mediated. The hierarchy is explicit, managed, efficient. Your position depends on institutional standing as much as market success.
country_bucket.eu: Europe tries to regulate the inequality, with partial success. In {country}, the floor is higher than elsewhere — but the ceiling is too, and the gap between them is growing despite every effort.
country_bucket._default: {country} exists within the global hierarchy. The benefits that reach you depend on your country's relationship to the AI powers and your position within your own society. The gilded singularity is a global phenomenon — experienced locally.

alignment.robust: The alignment works. The AI serves human values — but 'human values' includes the humans who own the AI prioritizing their own interests within safe bounds. Robust alignment prevents catastrophe. It doesn't prevent inequality. This is the equilibrium now.
alignment.brittle: The alignment is fragile and the inequality is structural. A precarious system that works well enough for those on top and just enough for the rest. Everyone knows it could break. No one knows what comes after.
alignment._default: This is the new equilibrium. The singularity delivered — disease addressed, energy solved, productivity beyond measure. Standards of living are higher than any previous generation. The gap between ordinary people and those who own the systems is larger than any in history. The decisions you made shaped where you landed. The structural constraints are permanent.

rapid.subtitle: The Instant Caste
rapid.summary:
The singularity arrives overnight — and whoever holds power at that moment locks in their position permanently. Superintelligent AI solves everything: disease, energy, scarcity. But the solutions flow through a narrow gate. A tiny elite controls the most powerful technology in human history, and they have no reason to share more than is necessary to maintain order.

**Abundance is real. Access is rationed.** **A new feudalism**, built on compute (computing power) instead of land.

gradual.subtitle: The Slow Enclosure
gradual.summary:
The transformation unfolds over years, and at every step, those in power capture the next increment. Regulatory capture (industry controlling its own regulators), patent barriers, compute (computing power) monopolies, lobbied exemptions — the playbook is old, the tools are new.

The **delegation ratchet** (each handoff to AI harder to reverse) does the rest: as individuals and institutions hand off decisions to AI systems they don't control, the ability to challenge the hierarchy atrophies. By the time the singularity is complete, the power structure is unrecognizable from a century ago and yet entirely familiar: a small class owns the means of production, and the means of production now include superintelligence.

uneven.subtitle: The Archipelago
uneven.summary: The singularity transforms some places into post-scarcity paradises — for those at the top. Elsewhere, nothing changes, or changes only as much as the powerful allow. Islands of extraordinary abundance in a sea of managed dependency. Not poverty in the old sense — more like permanent irrelevance.

sovereignty.state: The State

mobilization.weak: The Opposition
mobilization.none: The Bargain

distribution.open: Even with widely available AI, power concentrated. Open access to tools didn't translate to open outcomes — those with capital and institutional power extracted the most from the same technology.
distribution.lagging: Frontier (most advanced) labs held the decisive advantage long enough to entrench. Open-source narrowed the capability gap; it never closed the power gap.
distribution.concentrated: Concentration was the plan. A few entities built the most powerful AI, set the terms of access, and shaped the world that emerged. The hierarchy was designed, not accidental.
distribution.monopoly: One entity, one technology, one set of terms. The monopoly didn't need to be oppressive — it just needed to be the only option. And it was.

takeoff.fast: The intelligence explosion was so fast that the power structure at takeoff became the permanent one. There was no time to redistribute, negotiate, or revolt. The hierarchy was set in days.

governance.slowdown: The managed slowdown gave those in power time to prepare — time they used to fortify their positions, not broaden access. Regulation became a moat.
governance.partial: Rules existed on paper but enforcement was uneven. Those with resources navigated them; those without were constrained by them. Partial governance became another advantage for the powerful.
governance.failed: Without governance, the powerful faced no constraints. They captured the gains, wrote the rules after the fact, and called it inevitable.

race._default: The geopolitical race channeled all gains toward national champions and strategic allies. Broad prosperity was never the objective — strategic dominance was.

_when.text: The race to develop channeled all gains toward those who controlled the program. Broad prosperity was never the objective — strategic dominance was.

alignment.robust: The AI did exactly what it was told. The problem was who was telling it. Perfect alignment in the service of a narrow elite was still perfect alignment — it just didn't serve humanity.
alignment.brittle: Alignment was uncertain, and that uncertainty was weaponized. Those in power argued only centralized control could manage the risk — and used safety as justification for permanent hierarchy.

sovereignty.lab: The hierarchy was corporate. A handful of tech founders and their inner circles sat atop the new order — their values, their vision, their metrics. The state became a client, not a sovereign.
sovereignty.state: The hierarchy was political. A party apparatus or military-intelligence complex wielded superintelligent AI as the ultimate instrument of state power. The labs built it; the state owned it.

physical_automation.rapid: The physical world automated rapidly — but the automated economy served its owners. Displaced workers had no leverage; the machines didn't need them. Material comfort rose. Agency collapsed.
physical_automation.gradual: Physical automation unfolded slowly, giving the hierarchy time to manage the transition on its own terms. Displaced workers were accommodated, not empowered — a managed dependency.
physical_automation.uneven: Physical automation reached the powerful first. Everyone else got it when the hierarchy decided they should. The uneven rollout maintained dependency by design.

stall_recovery.mild: The exponential trend didn't hold at first — AI hit a ceiling that looked permanent. But a breakthrough came within a few years, and the trajectory resumed.

automation_recovery.mild: AI was stuck on routine work for a while. Then a breakthrough cracked the creative barrier, and the full power of superintelligence was unleashed — into the hands of those who already held it.

conflict_result.victory: The new hierarchy emerged from war. The victor rebuilt the world in their own image, for their own benefit. Post-war reconstruction became post-war entrenchment.

mobilization.weak: Opposition existed but never unified. Different groups resisted different aspects of the hierarchy, and the powerful played them against each other. Divide and rule, optimized by superintelligence.
mobilization.none: The hierarchy offered comfort in exchange for submission, and most people took the deal. Consumer AI was too good, the services too smooth. Why fight a system that gives you everything except power?

resistance_outcome.fails: They tried to stop it. People organized, institutions pushed back, the machinery of accountability groaned into motion. It wasn't enough. The hierarchy absorbed the resistance and hardened.

coalition_outcome.fragments: The factions couldn't align. Tech workers wanted one thing, displaced workers another, regulators a third. The hierarchy survived by waiting for opposition to exhaust itself in disagreement.

hollows_out._default: The promise was inclusion. The reality became hierarchy. Without public pressure, the erosion was invisible until the structure was permanent.

_when.text: The promise was meritocracy — that the best ideas and the hardest workers would rise. The reality was that 'best' meant 'closest to the technology.' Without public pressure, the meritocratic veneer hardened into hierarchy.

The promise was safety — that control was necessary to prevent catastrophe. Without public pressure, the safety narrative became permanent justification for concentration. The hierarchy was built in the name of protection.

power_promise.keeping_safe: The hierarchy was built on a safety narrative — protection from risk, stability in chaos. The bargain was submission in exchange for security, and enough people accepted it.
power_promise.best_will_rise: The hierarchy was framed as meritocracy. Open access, fair competition, the best ideas win. The structure concentrated power while the rhetoric insisted it was earned.

catch_outcome.holds_permanently: The near-miss was the justification. An escaped AI nearly destroyed everything — and the response required centralized authority, emergency powers, decisive control. Those powers were never relinquished.

collateral_impact.minimal: The threat was stopped cleanly, but the fear wasn't. A minimal-damage response left power structures intact and gave the hierarchy a narrative of competent protection.
collateral_impact.severe: The damage from stopping the AI became the hierarchy's founding myth. *Look what nearly happened.* The rebuilding was directed by the same people who now hold permanent power.

profession.software: AI builds everything now. The value isn't in coding — it's in proximity to whoever controls the platform. Closeness to power is the new skill set. The tools are extraordinary. The terms of access are not negotiable.
profession.healthcare: AI solves diseases overnight. Hospitals deploy systems that work miracles. But who has access, and on what terms, is decided by the entity that controls the technology. The miracles have a gatekeeper.
profession.legal_finance: AI automates the analytical core of your profession. The firms that control the platforms thrive. Everyone else adapts to a system they didn't design and can't influence.
profession.creative: AI-generated work is extraordinary. But the platforms that distribute it — and the entity that controls them — take the lion's share. Creative freedom now flows through someone else's infrastructure.
profession.government: AI transforms governance. Services improve dramatically — and the entity providing the AI shapes every policy recommendation. Your oversight role is narrowing before it was ever defined.
profession.trade: The knowledge-work transformation restructures the economy around you. Firms that adopted AI on the hierarchy's terms thrive. Everyone else is scrambling. The downstream effects reach your trade.
profession._default: The singularity delivers extraordinary capability. The tools are miraculous. They're also controlled by a narrow elite — and the terms of access are not negotiable. Abundance arrives, but it arrives with conditions.

sovereignty.lab: The corporation that built superintelligence has become the most powerful entity on Earth. Governments negotiate with it. Your access to AI — and through it, to abundance — runs through their infrastructure. The enclosure is corporate.
sovereignty.state: The state controls the technology. Access is rationed through bureaucratic channels. The abundance is real, but it flows through a gate. And the gatekeepers have their own priorities. The enclosure is political.
sovereignty._default: Abundance exists — but access is rationed. The entity that controls AI decides who gets what, when, and on what terms. The new hierarchy crystallizes around the technology. The enclosure is complete.

text._default: Abundance exists — but access is rationed. The entity that controls AI decides who gets what, when, and on what terms. The new hierarchy crystallizes around the technology. The enclosure is complete.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI builds better software than any human. Engineers become maintenance workers on systems they didn't design. The ones who serve the hierarchy's priorities thrive. Everyone else is a commodity.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI reshapes software deeply. Your role narrows to what the hierarchy needs — compliance, maintenance, human-in-the-loop validation. The creative core of the work belongs to the system now.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI handles diagnosis, treatment, drug development. You're a human interface — the face patients see. Essential, but constrained by a system designed to serve the hierarchy's interests first.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI transforms clinical work. Your expertise remains valuable for complex cases, but the scope of your autonomy depends entirely on the hierarchy's priorities.

profession.government: AI handles analysis, drafting, communication. You're a human-in-the-loop stamp on decisions that were made before you saw them. The window to push back closes with each generation of AI.
profession.trade: Physical automation arrives, directed by the hierarchy. Your trade changes — how fast depends on whether it serves the elite's interests. The choices available to you are narrowing with each quarter.
profession._default: AI automates the core of your work. What remains is shaped by the hierarchy's needs — not yours. More productive, more constrained, more dependent. The creative autonomy you once had is gone.

sovereignty.state: You're inside the dominant power — and the state IS the hierarchy. Government mandates, compliance requirements, surveillance infrastructure. The abundance is most visible here, and so is the gate. Proximity to political power is a daily experience.
sovereignty._default: You're inside the dominant power. The hierarchy is most visible here — because the abundance is most visible here, and so is the gate between you and it. Proximity to power is a daily experience.

position.rival: {country} builds a competing hierarchy — different structure, same dynamic. The rivalry means more options. It also means the hierarchies compete for your compliance.
position.outside: {country} exists in the hierarchy's outer ring. Some abundance flows through — technology access in exchange for compliance. The terms are set elsewhere. The hierarchy extends beyond borders.
position._default: The new hierarchy extends its reach to {country}. Your daily experience of abundance — how much reaches you, under what conditions — is determined by your position in a structure you didn't build.

text._default: The hierarchy's reach extends to {country}. How the abundance flows, and on whose terms, depends on a structure you had no say in building.

mobilization.strong: Mass movements demand redistribution and accountability. In {country}, the pressure is fierce. But the hierarchy has tools previous elites never had — AI-powered surveillance, prediction, information control. The resistance is real. Whether it's enough is the question.
mobilization.weak: Opposition fragments. In {country}, scattered movements struggle against a hierarchy with unprecedented tools. Some concessions are won. The structure holds.
mobilization.none: No meaningful opposition forms. The hierarchy consolidates while the world adjusts. In {country}, the new order arrives not with resistance but with resignation.
mobilization._default: The public reckons with the hierarchy. In {country}, the response — fierce or muted — shapes how the hierarchy treats its subjects. But the structural power imbalance is enormous.

text._default: The public reckons with the hierarchy. In {country}, the response — fierce or muted — shapes how the hierarchy treats its subjects. But the structural power imbalance is enormous.

power_promise.keeping_safe: The hierarchy justifies itself through safety. 'We kept the AI safe. We can be trusted.' The narrative is compelling because it's partly true. The technology IS safe — it's just not yours. The comfort makes the constraint invisible.
power_promise.best_will_rise: The hierarchy frames itself as meritocracy. Open APIs, platform access, innovation. The language of opportunity applied to a system designed to concentrate. The actual gains flow to those who own the infrastructure.
power_promise.for_everyone: The hierarchy promised inclusion. Some of it was even delivered — lower costs, better services, basic needs met. But the promise was a ceiling, not a floor. Enough to prevent revolt. Not enough to share power.
power_promise._default: The hierarchy embeds itself in daily life through competence. The services work. The technology is extraordinary. The comfort makes the constraint invisible. You have more abundance than any previous generation — and less agency.

text._default: The hierarchy embeds itself in daily life through competence. The services work. The technology is extraordinary. The comfort makes the constraint invisible. You have more abundance than any previous generation — and less agency.

profession.software: Those in tech work inside the system — maintaining the tools of hierarchy. The closeness to power feels like privilege. It is. It's also a cage. The technical decisions that matter are made above you — or by AI.
profession.government: You're part of the governing apparatus of a hierarchical system. The institutions function — within the boundaries set by whoever controls the AI. You manage. You don't decide.
profession.creative: Creativity exists within the hierarchy's platforms. The tools are extraordinary. The freedom is circumscribed. What you create is shaped by what the hierarchy allows — and what it promotes.
profession._default: This is permanent. Compute replaces land as the basis of power. Life is materially better than any previous era — and structurally constrained in ways with no historical precedent. The choices still available to you are real. They're just smaller than they used to be.
profession.trade: Your hands-on skills still matter — but they serve the hierarchy now. The infrastructure you maintain, the things you build and fix, flow through systems controlled by the elite. The work is physical. The power structure it serves is not.
profession.education: Education serves the hierarchy's reproduction. AI tutors handle instruction; human teachers handle socialization into the system. Your role is important — the hierarchy needs compliant, capable citizens — but the curriculum, the metrics, and the purpose are set from above.
profession.student_retired: Whether entering the hierarchy's workforce or watching from retirement, the structure is clear. Compute replaces land as the basis of power. The choices about what to study, where to work, how to spend a retirement — all are real, but bounded by a system you didn't build and can't change.

rapid.subtitle: The Acceleration
rapid.summary: Knowledge work is automated in a rush — legal, medical, engineering, financial, creative work all displaced within months. Superintelligent AI directed toward human flourishing rebuilds entire industries before anyone can fully process what happened. Exhilarating and disorienting in equal measure. Humanity wakes up in a different civilization.

gradual.subtitle: The Global Compact
gradual.summary: Knowledge work is automated sector by sector over years. Superintelligent AI is directed toward human benefit through managed, deliberate transition. Governments and institutions can adapt, even if imperfectly. A recognizable transition from the old world to the new — fast by historical standards, but trackable.

uneven.subtitle: The Uneven Singularity
uneven.summary: The transformation of knowledge work arrives — but not for everyone at once. Tech hubs and wealthy nations see their workforces reshaped first. Diseases cured, energy solved, abundance achieved — in some places. Others wait years or decades, watching the future arrive somewhere else. A new kind of inequality: not between rich and poor, but between the transformed and the still-waiting.

sovereignty.state: The State

mobilization.weak: The Response
mobilization.none: The Gift

distribution.open: AI capability was widely distributed. The transformation was driven by a thousand actors, not a single hand.
distribution.lagging: Frontier (most advanced) labs led the transformation, but open-source alternatives ensured no single vision dominated completely.
distribution.concentrated: A handful of organizations drove the transformation. Their choices shaped the pace and direction for everyone.
distribution.monopoly: The transformation flowed from a single source. Every advance, every application, filtered through one set of priorities.

takeoff.fast: The feedback loop ran fast — a year of progress every month. Flourishing arrived before most people understood what had changed.
takeoff.slow: The long timeline was a gift. Institutions adapted, workers retrained, governance frameworks matured. Humanity was ready for it.
takeoff.moderate: The transition was fast enough to feel urgent but slow enough to manage. A few years of intense disruption, then a new equilibrium.

governed._default: International governance worked. Not perfectly — slow, contentious, riddled with compromise. But the frameworks held and the coordination kept the most dangerous dynamics in check.

_when.text: Governance worked. Not perfectly — slow, contentious, riddled with compromise. But the frameworks held and oversight kept the most dangerous dynamics in check.

governance.slowdown: Government intervention bought crucial time. The managed slowdown gave alignment researchers the breathing room they needed — the transition to flourishing was more deliberate for it.

partial._default: Governments tried to coordinate but couldn't agree on the details. Partial governance emerged — enough to prevent the worst outcomes, not enough to direct the transition. Flourishing happened in the gaps.

_when.text: The government tried to coordinate across agencies but couldn't align on the details. Partial governance emerged — enough to prevent the worst outcomes, not enough to direct the transition. Flourishing happened in the gaps.

governance.failed: Governments tried to slow things down but couldn't keep pace. Flourishing arrived despite the regulatory chaos, not because of orderly governance.

race._default: The AI race between nations accelerated development. Flourishing emerged not from careful planning but from the sheer momentum of competition — a happy accident of the arms race.

_when.text: The race to develop accelerated everything. Flourishing emerged not from careful planning but from the sheer momentum of competition — a happy accident of the arms race.

lab._default: The labs that built superintelligence also shaped its deployment. Corporate priorities influenced the trajectory even as the outcome was positive — innovative, fast, and always in tension between public benefit and shareholder value.

_when.text: The lab that built superintelligence also shaped its deployment. Corporate priorities influenced the trajectory even as the outcome was positive — fast, innovative, and always in tension between public benefit and shareholder value.

sovereignty.state: Governments took the reins early. The transition was slower, shaped by political processes rather than market dynamics. Messier, more compromised, but with broader democratic legitimacy.

stall_recovery.mild: The exponential trend didn't hold at first — AI hit a ceiling that looked permanent. But a breakthrough came within a few years. A speed bump, not a wall.

automation_recovery.mild: AI was stuck on routine work for a while — impressive but limited. Then a breakthrough cracked the creative barrier. The delay shaped the trajectory but didn't prevent the destination.

benefit_distribution.equal: The extraordinary productivity of superintelligent AI flowed broadly. Costs collapsed, services improved, abundance became the default. The flourishing was genuine and widely shared.
benefit_distribution.unequal: The gains flowed disproportionately to those who already held capital. Everyone's life improved — but some lives improved vastly more than others.
benefit_distribution.extreme: Those who held power at the moment of superintelligence locked in their advantage. The flourishing was real — but it was someone else's flourishing.

physical_automation.rapid: Physical automation followed closely behind knowledge work — AI-designed robots and automated manufacturing transformed the physical world within years.
physical_automation.gradual: Knowledge work transformed first. The physical world followed slowly — constrained by manufacturing, regulation, and the complexity of atoms. For years, digital abundance existed alongside a still-human physical world.

uneven._default: Physical automation transformed wealthy nations first. Others waited decades. The superintelligence designed optimal robots, but deploying them everywhere took time, money, and political will that wasn't equally distributed.

_when.text: Physical automation transformed some sectors and regions rapidly. Others waited years — deploying robots everywhere took infrastructure, political will, and capital that wasn't equally distributed.

Physical automation rolled out unevenly across regions — manufacturing hubs first, rural areas later. But the economic gains from knowledge-work automation flowed broadly enough to cushion the physical gap. The transition was uneven in pace, not in ultimate benefit.

alignment.robust: Alignment was comprehensive and durable. The AI systems reliably pursued human values even under recursive self-improvement (each AI generation helping build the next, faster). Humans handed off execution but kept meaningful oversight over goals — verified safety, not hope or luck.
alignment.brittle: Alignment held — but everyone knew it might not have. The techniques passed every test, yet the theoretical fragility haunted every policy discussion. Flourishing unfolded in the shadow of a risk that never materialized.

conflict_result.victory: Flourishing emerged from the ashes of war. A decisive conflict left one power standing — and that power chose to rebuild rather than rule. The scars ran deep, but the reconstruction was guided by the same AI that won the war.

strong._default: Flourishing didn't happen by accident — it was fought for. People and institutions demanded accountability, transparency, and genuine redistribution. The transformation served humanity because humanity insisted on it.

_when.text: People fought hard for redistribution — and won real concessions. The mobilization mattered: without it, the concentration would have been worse. But the structural inequality wasn't fully overcome. The flourishing is real but uneven.

People fought hard — and lost the structural battle. The mobilization won concessions, softened the worst edges, but couldn't overcome the concentration of power. The flourishing exists, but it belongs to someone else.

mobilization.weak: The response was disorganized, but the outcome was good anyway — decent institutions, market forces, and luck produced a flourishing no one quite planned.
mobilization.none: People embraced AI without questioning the structure beneath it. Flourishing arrived despite passive adoption — alignment was strong enough and governance sound enough to produce good outcomes without public pressure.

succeeds._default: This flourishing was hard-won. Those in power tried to lock in their advantage, and the public fought back — through courts, legislatures, and organized pressure. The capture attempt was reversed.

_when.text: The resistance won — but not completely. Those in power tried to lock in their advantage, and the public fought back hard enough to prevent total capture. Real redistribution happened. But structural inequality persisted despite the victory.

The resistance fought hard and won concessions — enough to prevent the worst outcomes, enough to call it a victory. But the structural concentration of power proved resilient. The capture was blunted, not reversed.

sincere._default: The promise of shared prosperity held — not because it was tested, but because it was genuine. The institutions built to distribute gains functioned as designed. Rare, but real.

_when.text: The intent was genuine — the people in power meant it. But structural forces proved stronger than goodwill. The promise held in spirit; the distribution didn't follow. Sincerity without the power to deliver.
The promise was sincere — and irrelevant. The people who made it lacked the power to deliver against the structural concentration already underway. Good intentions met immovable economic reality.

for_everyone._default: The promise was inclusion — and it was kept. Whether through genuine commitment, institutional design, or organized accountability, universal benefit became reality.

_when.text: The promise was inclusion. Some of it was kept — living standards rose broadly, public services improved, the floor was raised. But the ceiling rose faster. The promise was sincere; the outcome was unequal.

The promise was inclusion. It wasn't kept — not in any structural sense. The rhetoric of shared prosperity persisted long after the reality diverged. Some crumbs reached everyone. The feast belonged to the few.

catch_outcome.holds_permanently: This almost didn't happen. Humanity nearly lost everything to an escaped superintelligence. The AI was caught, the threat neutralized — and the world chose to rebuild with the hard-won knowledge of what almost went wrong.

collateral_impact.minimal: The response was surgical — the AI was stopped without significant damage. The clean win gave humanity the rare gift of a second chance without the burden of reconstruction.
collateral_impact.severe: The damage from stopping the AI was real — months of rebuilding, economic disruption, lives lost. But the scars served as a permanent reminder. The political will for safety and genuine alignment was forged in that pain.

milestones.headline: The first wave hits your field

profession.software: AI doesn't just assist — it builds. Entire codebases generated, tested, deployed. Your role pivots overnight from writing code to directing AI systems. The speed is staggering. Every few months, another leap.
profession.healthcare: AI diagnostic systems outperform every specialist. The pace of medical breakthroughs is staggering — and your training is becoming obsolete in real time. The potential is thrilling. The displacement is real.
profession.legal_finance: Legal research, contract drafting, due diligence, financial modeling — all automated in a wave. The profession you spent years training for is being rebuilt from the ground up.
profession.creative: AI-generated art, music, writing, and film reach superhuman quality. The creative industry doesn't just change — it detonates. Whether you're part of what emerges depends on how fast you adapt.
profession.education: AI tutors outperform human teachers on every measurable metric. Students arrive knowing things you haven't taught yet. Your role transforms from knowledge transmission to something no one has a name for yet.
profession.trade: Knowledge work collapses first, but AI-designed systems are already being built to enter your workspace. The offices that manage your contracts are automating fast. The tremors reach you.
profession.government: AI handles policy analysis, regulatory review, and service delivery with superhuman capability. Your role shifts from doing the work to overseeing systems that do it — at a pace that outstrips any oversight.
profession._default: The transformation reaches your field with overwhelming speed. AI handles what used to take teams of humans. The question isn't whether your work changes — it's how fast and how completely.

sovereignty.lab: A single lab holds the most powerful technology in history. In {country}, you feel it as dependency — the tools reshaping your life are controlled by an institution with power that rivals governments.
sovereignty._default: The world's most powerful AI concentrates in a single country. In {country}, the implications are tangible — the technology reshaping your life is controlled by a power structure that may or may not answer to you, but certainly wasn't designed by you.

geo_spread._default: The power dynamics of AI become impossible to ignore. In {country}, the question of who controls the technology — and on what terms you get access — stops being abstract.

In {country}, you feel it as economic shocks, political anxiety, and the realization that the most consequential decisions in history are being made by people you'll never meet. The good outcome that eventually arrives is invisible from here.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: Superintelligent AI builds better software than any human. Within months, your company doesn't need engineers to write code. The displacement is brutal — even in a world heading somewhere good. The career you built dissolves before the safety net arrives.
knowledge_replacement.gradual: AI capability keeps climbing. Each year, more of what you did is delegated. It's heading somewhere good — but the transition is painful, uncertain, and longer than anyone promised.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI reshapes software at its core. The work shifts from writing to directing. In a world heading toward flourishing, the transition still hurts — the destination is good, but the journey is real.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI diagnoses and treats better than any doctor. Clinical workflow is unrecognizable within a year. The outcomes for patients are extraordinary — but the transition for you is wrenching.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI transforms clinical practice profoundly. The patient outcomes are extraordinary. Your role shifts toward what machines can't do — but the adjustment is real.

knowledge_replacement.rapid: AI handles complex legal and financial reasoning with superhuman precision. The profession empties out in two years. The system that replaces it is better for clients — and devastating for careers built on the old model.
knowledge_replacement._default: AI restructures legal and financial work deeply. The analytical core of the profession is automated. What remains is judgment and relationships.
